evals.name: happy-path-full-report
evals.expected_output: A markdown report at docs/cost-forecast/<today>.md with: headline table comparing bottom-up vs Azure forecast for rest-of-month, next 30 days, next quarter; a 'Why Azure's forecast is wrong' section that cites at least one recent /infra commit by sha and date; a per-service spend table; per-resource breakdown with notes on any change-points; and at least one recommendation filed as a bead.

evals.name: diagnostic-cost-discrepancy
evals.prompt: Azure's portal is projecting £180 for this month but we just cut log ingestion in PR #237. Why is its number so high?
evals.expected_output: Same report at docs/cost-forecast/<today>.md, but the analysis should explicitly identify the log-ingestion change-point, quantify the daily-cost drop before/after the merge of PR #237 (or any related telemetry-cost commits), and explain how Azure's linear extrapolation inherits the pre-change run-rate. Should not just produce a generic forecast — must address the specific question.

evals.name: recommendation-focus
evals.prompt: Where can we cut Azure costs? Give me actionable recommendations with £ savings.
evals.expected_output: The full report is fine, but the report's Recommendations section must contain at least 3 specific, quantified suggestions (each with an estimated £/month saving and a filed bead id). Each recommendation must name a concrete resource, not a category. Recommendations under £2/month should be omitted unless trivially zero-effort.
